title: Showing dependence lines in GANTT view is exported to PDF
excerpt: When you are exporting the Gantt view to PDF format, the dependence lines are not showing. This is confuse because in board view the dependence lines are there.

posts.author: Patricia (Patricia Romero)
posts.cookedHtml: When you are exporting the Gantt view to PDF format, the dependence lines are not showing. This is confuse because in board view the dependence lines are there.

posts.author: klegge (Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that this should be a standard feature of the gantt export to pdf functionality. Otherwise there is no point even having an option to show dependencies if you’re not then able to present this in plans to exec level stakeholders.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Patricia and @klegge ,

if you use PDFs for collaboration on timelines with clients, contractors, or other third parties outside of monday.com, I want to mention a more efficient way to do this on monday.

We’ve noticed the following pattern: Export Gantt → attach PDF → send email → wait for feedback → update timeline → export again → resend.

Repeat. And repeat. And repeat.

Somewhere along the way:

• no one remembers which version is the latest
• timeline changes get buried in long emails
• approvals take days
• testing gets delayed
• last-minute pressure before launch becomes inevitable

We developed the Interactive Gantt Timeline app, that allows to share Gantt with clients and collaborate directly on it:

  • Clients can leave comments and suggest timeline changes right on Gantt

    leave suggestions2045×1045 312 KB

  • PMs get notification about comments and suggestions, and can test different scenarios, and then approve the appropriate timeline.

    test versions gantt1581×993 212 KB

After approving, new timeline automatically updates the board.

Interactive Gantt Timeline doesn’t replace monday.com’s native Gantt — it enhances it.
Keep using monday.com’s Gantt view internally, and share Interactive Gantt Timeline with clients outside your workspace.

No version chaos.
No endless PDF exports.
Just one live Gantt for monday.com.

You can install and test the app on your account.
